title: Mixer Initialization, Channels, Latency, Volume, Mute, and Cue Alternatives · Changed-Input Lab
summary: An independent adaptive-arcade team receives a different viewport, device, frame rate, seed, entity count, accessibility profile, platform, and delivery constraint and transfers mixer initialization, channels, latency, volume, mute, and cue alternatives into a changed bounded accessible audio and cue system. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of bounded accessible audio and cue system with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, expli…
objectives: Configure mixer frequency, format, channels, buffer, and initialization before loading sounds and record platform limits.
Reserve or prioritize channels for critical cues and define overlap, stealing, cooldown, and device-loss behavior.
Provide master and separate effects and music control, mute, remembered settings, and safe defaults.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Mixer Init
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab Asteroids team handles case 8122 during a keyboard-only community arcade night. Build deterministic Python evidence to own mixer initialization, channel budgets, latency, volume and mute controls, and non-audio alternatives for every critical cue; test empty, zero-vector, seam, low-frame-rate, focus-loss, device-loss, missing-asset, corrupted-save, and rejected cases; then lose window focus while thrust and fire are held, then prove both actions release safely. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Configure mixer frequency, format, channels, buffer, and initialization before loading sounds and record platform limits.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Mixer Init.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for configure mixer frequency, format, channels, buffer, and initialization before loading sounds and record platform limits. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Mixer Init, keep Mixer Init connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Audio Failure
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: asteroids-audio-failure-1vhtly" that uses case 6105 to own mixer initialization, channel budgets, latency, volume and mute controls, and non-audio alternatives for every critical cue. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not claim native pygame-ce, SDL, window, controller, mixer, filesystem, package, GPU, installer, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, released pygame-ce 2.5.7, fixed and headless SDL fixtures, real devices, licensed assets, accessibility playtests, profiling, clean installations, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Audio Failure.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Audio Failure into observable performance evidence. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Audio Failure, keep Audio Failure connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-audio-failure-1vhtly
# Competency: asteroids-audio-failure.
# Case 6105: an offline cross-platform release rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: keep every critical cue available through more than color or sound and every action remappable without timing precision.
# Changed case: lose window focus while thrust and fire are held, then prove both actions release safely.
def evidence_asteroids_audio_failure_1vhtly(critical_events: int = 5, visual_alternatives: int = 5, channels_reserved: int = 3, clipped_sounds: int = 0, mute_available: bool = True, separate_volume: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1vhtly = "1vhtly-ylthv1-6105"
    assert evidence_variant_1vhtly
    evidence_axes_1vhtly = (
        "player1vhtly",
        "seed6105",
        "inputylthv1",
        "frame1vhtly6105",
        "state61051vhtly",
        "playtestylthv16105",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1vhtly)) == 6
    if critical_events < 0 or channels_reserved < 1:
        return False, critical_events, "audio-domain"
    if visual_alternatives != critical_events:
        return False, visual_alternatives, "cue-alternative"
    if clipped_sounds > 0:
        return False, clipped_sounds, "channel-starvation"
    if not mute_available or not separate_volume:
        return False, channels_reserved, "audio-control"
    assert visual_alternatives == critical_events
    return True, channels_reserved, "accessible-audio"
